title: Reichus dissection of the ReiQ reveal scene
display_title: Reichus dissection of the ReiQ reveal scene
wikitext:
[]: Reichu's dissection of the ReiQ reveal scene             Quote Post by Kendrix » Sat Dec 19, 2020 3:29 pm Now while Reichu's interpretations are at times far fetched & I disagree with her takes on some of the characters, she's very good at picking up meaningful details practically when it comes to the EVAs/angels/ "magic systems" & we MUST absolutely honor & appreciate how she took the time to do this. Regarding part 1 I think I've already noted how Fuyutsuki's relationship to Yui seems to be the same in Rebuild (HE "stubbornly held onto" a photo that didn't have Gendo in it) Also it explains what the headless Rei's are; So far my impression was that they're collected from dead clones for science or something, but they are actually newest generation dummy plugs (indeed I just realized there's even two lines of them) - the structure is called a " manufacturing plant ". Both a parallel to ep 23, but also another example of how the "artificial soldier" related horror has been amped up here. They only need the heads, the brains... everything else has been dispensed with as uneccesary. Also, they obviously tie in with the various EVA component parts around Neo Nerv and the "green army" of EVAs in the trailer. Indeed in a cut scene dissected in the second part it's show that there's even more factory equipment in that room. Capsules & conveyor belts to box the finished dummy plugs. I feel proud of myself for including a factory of partially disassembled Reis in a fanfic now. A depressing thing that would have come through more if that had stayed in tho, is that it's not that Fuyutsuki has the Rei heads. in his chess room. He has set up his chess table in the Rei factory. Consider that visual. Consider an old man living in the ruins of his foolish dream. New information from the second part: -ReiQ is clone number six Pity, I headcannoned her as number nine. You know, because 'Q' is pronounced the same as kyuu? But again, interim Rei's can be deduced - she was sent & prepared by SEELE. This too can actually be deduced. Mark 9 is clearly SEELE's toy, and Mari calls her "SEELE pilot" though she could've been lumping Gendo & SEELE together. For the most part, Gendo didn't have any tools/pilots left that weren't foreign tools sent to fulfill SEELE's agenda. I was always surprised that we never got a version of "Q with a few little extra scenes added" like they did for the first two movies. My guess is that, just like they're holding out on the Q CR, they don't want to drop their "punchline". But I expect that IF it did happen, the extra bits of Fuyutsuki's explanation might have been included. I hope it will be. Now a lot of this probably still applies, it looks like a time/ centering cut. The existence of "interim Reis" is already heavily implied through that school uniform next to ReiQ's room. Then there's the very ambiguous use of the word"data". When Fuyutsuki said that "her data remains in the Rei clones" one would at first think that he's simply referring to her genes. It could just be some private desire of Gendo's to replicate her, or just a means (at least in the original, they didn't choose Yui as a donor; They used her lillith-contaminated "corpse", literally "salvaged remains". ) Maybe it was just as a means to "program" her at all, better copy something existent than build from scratch. Like how Naoko used herself as a base for the Magi. We've certainly heard that both the Magis and the Dummy plugs were a result of research into "artificial soul" But that's the original. If data doesn't just mean genes, what is it? Because it's not Yui's soul, she's in EVA 01. So far I've always been a proponent of the interpretation that Rei is ultimately meant to be her own person & in terms of character completely independent, a new person molded by a different life & environment - certainly her actions in wanting to make sure Shinji never has to pilot again vs. Yui making sure he absolutely does have to by refusing to accept the Dummy seem different enough. Still I must admit that. Though Rei could still have become her own person after starting with a similar "basis programming/temperament" (irl where there is no eva specific magic, genes would already include base temperament), and that they tried to program her to be similar doesn't mean they necessarily succeeded to create more than an artists' impression of a Yui-like human personality. I don't even think the memory transfer made the original 3 "the same", but it could pertain to how her relationships with others are to be seen. For example the line of argument that "Shinji didn't befriend her because she resembled Yui, after all ReiQ is still a clone of her to the same extent" becomes a bit less certain now Did Yui undergo a brain scan/"tube session", or is that "data" something they got from her remains? It should be noted that all the pilots, even still living ones, leave some imprint that makes the EVAs "smell" like them (though since the sense of smell is the most connected to the unconscioous/emotions, itmight be something telepathic being "parsed" as a smell. ) and that the EVAs need to be configured for the pilot's "profiles". That's my best guess, if I had to chose one - they still had that "profile" from the contact experiment that that somehow went into Rei's "programming". Perhaps out of sentimentality, perhaps because they had to use something to get more than oversized infant. In the original series Rei was originally supposed to be EVA 01's pilot but in Rebuild Shinji was explicitly always intended as a pilot because of the whole "destiny manipulating" But leaving the question of how Yui is related to it aside for now (since we will probably see the intention from how things are framed in final), what's out of question is that they didn't bother programming ReiQ to the same extent Either SEELE wanted her to be more controllable, doesn't need her to show initiative since their program in Mark 9 will do that, or they had no need of anything Gendo added out of sentiment (though I still think that whatever the underlying undercurrent he must have had some technological reason to rationalize it with) Even in NGE the memory storage per tube didn't really transfer the complete personality, & its artificiality was apparent in how it only went back to the last "save point" - so my theory re: how ReiQ is different from of Rei III (and they are very different indeed; Superficially they're both at first glance significantly less expressive than even early series Rei II, but if I had to describe them with two words, Rei III would be "angry ghost" and ReiQ "lost child") was just signal degradation. Copy of a copy of a copy... each time more unreliable. Something is left but not much - I based on this on how she seemed to have some idea who he is, & addresses him the same way, but it's only a grainy primitive recognition, more factual memory than biographic. But of course it's fully possible that Gendo just told her Shinji's name & she didn't have a preexisting notion of him at all, or, Seele thought basic knowledge his name/appearance could be left in. In NGE terms, it's like they decanted a clone and didn't load in the memories, or only a fraction of them. Certainly the most interesting part is where Reichu discusses two possible interpretations for the line relating to Gendo that he translated differently than Number-kun. "She doesn't have Yui's data so Gendo isn't interested" implies something waaaay different than "“Ikari can’t muster up enough interest in this clone to bother putting Yui’s data into it” The former is pretty 1D. Gendo is super callous, interested only in Yui, doesn't care about Rei and certainly not a Rei that's an even worse copy of her. there would even be the possibility to read this as implying that Shinji is basically the same as Gendo here (though for me, many more data points point to it instead being a validation of his "no one can replace you" line, especially with how the books come into it. Of course if we learn tomorrow that Yui was actually a huge bookworm, then it's time for me to redo my interpretation of the movies from scratch and probably pick a new favorite character.) The second one, however, is MUCH more... it's - It's so VERY Gendo-like , so much in line with how I've always understood him. And that's the main reason I wish this had been left in since there's little else, besides that one office conversation, that provides any in Gendo, to begin with, is a character whose life is essentially "on hold" because he's never accepted Yui's death; Everything will be justified & fixed through instrumentality, so whatever he does in its name doesn't matter. He was jaded & somewhat overwhelmed by interpersonal matters to begin with, and the loss destroyed him - He can only pull up so much interest or emotional energy for the present. Not for Ritsuko, not for his children. He's friendly to Rei but it's still somewhat distant, faded, he doesn't really know what goes on in her head. He didn't choose to get attached to Rei, he couldn't help it because he's still made up of human brain chemicals. This is why I don#t really think he made Rei more similar to Yui on purpose, he doesn't do reminders he burned them all and he hopes to get the original from eva 01. He created her from the "salvaged remains" because that's the human SoL amalgam sample that he had. In 2.0 that seemed to be changing a bit, he was actually interacting with Shinji... But now like in all the other relationships, all the progress was erased. The timeskip must have been tough for Gendo too after all at the end of 2.22 he thought that it was "only a little longer". That didn't work out for him. At all. Unlike Misato & Asuka who--- yes they became bitter & didn't show their best sides to Shinji but aside from his specific case they are heroic defenders of humanity. They grew from their loss. Gendo has become even more callous, even colder, even more "dehumanized" butmostly even more burned out emotionally. He doesn't even try building new bonds. Perhaps the last few clones with the programming did have "copy of a copy" signal degradation; In any case he didn't bother to try again. He can't bring himself to care, he's reduced to apathy, it would be too painful, maybe. That's also why he only speaks with Shinji as briefly as possible. Interacting with him would also be too painful. Besides he's still going to use them so, better not get attached to the tools. The suggestion of other replicas is certainly a good catch, that might explain the Kaworu shots from the trailer Especially with the revelation that the fourth part is essentially two films in one, it seems the originally planned "third part" balooned and was split into two. (they weren't even sure where to make the cut, hence the existence of "until you come to me"); Or maybe the cut is where it is because Anno needed a break. Still, "right after Kaworu's death, ending with the trio wandering into the landscape" is a good, logical end for Q. The PLAQUE is also interesting & might suspect that there were actually a whole lot more If Mark six was piloted by another Rei that explains why the angel takes a Rei-ish form later on . (To ReiQ's horror) Or maybe that's the designation of the clone in its dummy plug (since Mark 6 is "autonomous"), so maybe not all of these were active Reis. Especially since "00" is assigned to "01" (remember the classic series dummy plug labeled "Rei 00"?) I'm not the only one who has speculated in fanfic about a non-functional protope "Rei 0". "Rei Infinity" is definitely the new head-only dummy plugs. Ep 23 had a panel listing all theclones from 4 to 24 and Rirsuko uses it to destroy them. One Rei has definitely made it to Lillith in some form. I mean, this would also explain/ mention the missing EVAs between 5 and 12 excluding 8. Also, I Think the R"Rei" in front of 00 is simply in uppercase, and those two dots are a badly written lowercase i. Or maybe they got lazy in the scrubbing and it would have all been written the same. Or, it's not Mark Six. It's Eva Six, the Marks & regular EVAs actually not having the same numbering. I'd though they just threw in extra EVAs and Reis tolampshade that much time has passed and that we'll never hear of them; just I didn't expect a clear rundown of what exactly happened (some time passed between NTI and actual TI, but EVA 01 must have been involved because of all the EVA 01 shaped things produced. ) Anyways, this is a goldmine, thanks Reichu, even if we don't get full answers we'll have fun speculating. Someone must try their hand at the timeskip chaos. Though that would be a lot like "EoTv but the physical events". Indeed I'm not even sure that the circle really means "active". We'd know if we could know how 06 looks. If it's circled it's active; If it's just written there without cross outs or circles, circles mean something else. Strikethrough is probably "dead", like Rei I. (Perhaps the long string of consecutive dead ones with no EVAs assigned had something to do with Ritsuko's defection to WILLE hehe) Circle could be " sealed ". So the list originally started out with no circles or strikethroughs. Rei 2 and the Mark Six Rei (if she existed) were both sealed/merged. (this would also fit the idea that Mark 9-like EVAs absorb their pilots, this happened to some other Reis, hence the circle, because they're now sealed. ) I'm so hype friends

[]: Re: Reichu's dissection of the ReiQ reveal scene             Quote Post by Derantor » Sat Dec 19, 2020 3:38 pm Might be good to include a link to the dissection in question, for context: Secrets of the Ayanami Series Pt. 1 Secrets of the Ayanami Series Pt. 2

[]: Re: Reichu's dissection of the ReiQ reveal scene             Quote Post by Kendrix » Mon Dec 21, 2020 3:20 pm Oh, another thing I just noticed: The script does not actually describe "Mari's mother" or whoever the woman with the barette is. Perhaps adding her might've been a late decision. Pity, because how the stage directions describe her might have been telling. Then again that might be precisely why they weren't added or published.

[]: Re: Reichu's dissection of the ReiQ reveal scene             Quote Post by dzzthink » Sat Dec 26, 2020 7:23 am This begs the question of what the Yui data was even for and its purpose. It could be that its inherent nature of providing Yui's maternal 'smell' or familiarity is what makes Shinji so drawn to her in the first place, and that this was an overarching ploy by Nerv to make Shinji become desperate to save her and trigger 4th impact in the 2nd film. We know smell is key to identifying people you know of and provides a framework for developing relationships. It also makes some sense considering that Yui data was not provided for ReiQ leading to her loss of identity feeling. It also explains why Gendo has no interest whatsoever in interacting with ReiQ, who does not possess any of Yui's imprint. I think the description Rei III would be "angry ghost" and ReiQ "lost child" is a perfect description of the Rei clones. In contrast to Rei Q, Rei III does probably still have Yui's data and this gives her an intrinsic sense of who everyone is and their relationship to her. In this sense, it is the Yui data that has provided her motivations to choose Shinji over Gendo in EoE. Indeed, it is probable that ReiQ will be needed to trigger the instrumentality in the Rebuild movies and the omission of Yui's data, as you have said, was done for SEELE to make her more controllable. However, it is worthy to point out that Rei Q and Rei III both share the power of being able to hear Shinji calling out to Rei since in 3.33 Rei Q was able to locate Shinji on the Wille ship. This leads to another more interesting theory that for the next incarnation of Reis after Rei II, it is Yui's maternal instincts/emotions combined with possessing Lillith qualities that give some metaphysical third-eye sense of those around them (especially Shinji). Overall, it would be interesting to understand how much the Lillith part of Rei gives rise to her being and personality. Another question is whether she was created only upon Shinji reawakening to retrieve him from Wille. This again makes it difficult to understand how a clone can be created or grown using the technology they have. Rather than the typical embryo growth from fetus to child to teenager, it is likely that the formation of Rei relies on more unorthodox approaches. This makes it more likely that Rei Q was created only using biological data as a guide and Lilith's material for the rest. Then this psychological data is sort of an add-on that could be left out if needed. I agree with the concept of Rei being her own person, no matter what the incarnation is, and it is the environmental experiences and not Yui data or DNA that forms her personality. This is referenced heavily in episode 25 during Rei's monologue where she states that who she is, is based on the relationships she has formed and the experiences she has had. Then when she is asked whether she is afraid of her true self, we get flashes of the dummy plug reis', which suggests her original self was essentially an empty shell with no personality. This also suggests that the process of developing Rei clones all start off with physical replicas but with blank mental states. She is essentially afraid of being reverted back to nothing and lose everything that she has gained in her life to form her personality of Rei II. This is quite a tragedy later on when she dies and is no longer able to continue as her true self and build on what her life has been. However, based on the assumption that Rei is her own person and doesn't necessarily share any identity with Yui, then what is it that exactly is this Yui-like sense that governs Rei III's decision to choose Shinji over Gendo in EoE? Well, I have been thinking about this recently and I believe, that it is not necessarily a genetic or personal decision of Rei III but a program or instinct. You see, unit 01 is not Yui nor does it act like a human, yet it acts on some basic impulse or program to protect Shinji whenever possible. Yui would never have the capacity to start behaving like a berserk monster and start eating an angel or tear them apart limb from limb, yet Unit 01 has the ability to act on a set of instructions guided by Yui's soul to do these violent acts. In the same way, although Rei III has never known Shinji or Gendo all that much, she acts out based on a program whether it be a soul or some set of unlocked genes (epigenetics even) that makes her do the things that she does without thought. It isn't necessarily a personal decision, but a natural reaction ordained by the possession of Yui's remaining psyche. I also admit there is another theory floating around about Rei III having Rei II's memory so make the decision to give the choice to Shinji, but I don't think this is necessarily accurate since Rei III seems to be different to Rei II (cracks gendo's glasses whilst Rei II during instrumentality picks up Gendo's glasses). There is also the manga (book 12 and 13), which also suggests Rei III remembering Rei II's interactions with Shinji and holding his hand but I'm not sure if they are following the same storyline or rather Sadamoto just added this for the sake of simplifying the Rei incarnation dynamics.

[]: Re: Reichu's dissection of the ReiQ reveal scene             Quote Post by Kendrix » Sat Dec 26, 2020 12:36 pm Rei III was already merged with Adam and EVA 01 in mid-ritual at that point tho so I don't think this is a power she would naturally have at any point in time, especially since she doesn't display it in any other situations, including some where it would have been really useful (ep 16 or 20 for example) As for the moment before ReiQ's arrival, that's mysterious indeed. There is the theory that it was actually Rei II within EVA 01 speaking or whichever "interim Rei" merged with Lillith, especially since we've seen random apparitions like that throughout, including the one that appeared to ReiQ in her tube. Since that's a mystery that had deliberate attention drawn to it (it's really lampshaded that Sakura and Misato can't hear the "telepathic" voice) this might still get an explicit resolution/explanation. Though at this point it's fully possible that ReiQ - or even all the Reis in the rebuild continuity - have telepathy as an explicit power. It would explain how Kaworu can talk on the airless surface of the moon, assuming their power set is still similar.

[]: Re: Reichu's dissection of the ReiQ reveal scene             Quote Post by JoelcrNeto » Mon Dec 28, 2020 9:29 am Reichu posted an “informal followup” like her said .

[]: Re: Reichu's dissection of the ReiQ reveal scene             Quote Post by Kendrix » Mon Dec 28, 2020 4:19 pm The "ReiQ is actually looking at the Program screen, not the angel" idea is interesting, as is the concept that she was the vessel for the Mk. 9s program (that part I'm inclined to believe actually, it does seem to be the implication in Fuyu's line) At this point Shinji reacting at her has managed to confuse her enough to jumpstart some self awareness but its at the level of a small kid. "What do i do, what do I do I'm confused " - "oh, this person is talking to me and giving me answers, I will help her" I'm not sure if I would call it "a refusal to sacrifice herself", seeing as she's letting Asuka get at the core. Rather what's apparent here is that she had no real understanding of the mission or any loyalty to either SEELE or NERV. She wouldn't smack you if you badmouthed them, she doesn't need convincing that you're bad. She's doing what she's told "because I say so" & the moment she gets the idea to do anything else at all the instructions are forgotten One might speculate that she would have been eaten/absorbed the moment the Mark Nine regrew its head/transformed, like Mari warned her, but I didn't see anything suggested that anyone would gain something like that. That refers to her emotional status, not her mental one, she seems to desire information (not baby-like at all) & that's probably also part of why she follows Asuka.She would probably "catch up" to her physical age eventually if she got properly cared for.

[]: Re: Reichu's dissection of the ReiQ reveal scene             Quote Post by Blockio » Mon Dec 28, 2020 11:44 pm Kendrix wrote: The "ReiQ is actually looking at the Program screen, not the angel" idea is interesting, as is the concept that she was the vessel for the Mk. 9s program (that part I'm inclined to believe actually, it does seem to be the implication in Fuyu's line)

[]: Re: Reichu's dissection of the ReiQ reveal scene             Quote Post by JoelcrNeto » Tue Dec 29, 2020 8:03 am Kendrix wrote: That refers to her emotional status, not her mental one, she seems to desire information (not baby-like at all) & that's probably also part of why she follows Asuka.She would probably "catch up" to her physical age eventually if she got properly cared for.

[]: Re: Reichu's dissection of the ReiQ reveal scene             Quote Post by dzzthink » Fri Jan 08, 2021 9:01 pm I want to stress again Reichu's previous analysis of 3.33 deleted scene about Yui's data being absent in Rei and Seele data being used instead. The question is why would the creators want to delete this important plot detail? I think it may have to do with not revealing seeles agenda so early on until shin eva and I want to draw a parallel with a similar deleted scene in the series, which is Kaworu's lake discussion with Seele. In that scene, we learn more about Seele's hidden agenda, as well as Kaworu's true identity as the vessel of Adam's soul. Seele also insists that they are on the angels side and consider them a more superior successor, almost as though to convince kaworu to go along with their plans. Kaworu, as we know, is Seele's golden boy, their last resort to thwarting Gendo's plans before they wage a full-on war against NERV in EoE. Just like how Gendo needs Rei to carry out third impact, Seele uses Kaworu for their own purposes by tricking him to think that it is adam in the geofront. Why Kaworu seems to prefer Adam over Lillith is a mystery (aside from adam being the progenitor of angels and lillith being the progenitor of humans) but we know that his death as the final Angel as well as the removal of the spear of Longinus is a crucial step for Gendo to carry out his agenda in EoE, much to seeles dismay. Overall, this deletion of the seele data dialogue may have been done to not give away seeles agneda and their potential deception/manipulation of other characters. In the series, there are two separate agendas working against each other: Nerv and Seele, with seele pulling much of the strings with kaworus manipulation. In the Rebuild, there are now three factions at war against each other: Wille, Seele, and Nerv. The lines from the deleted scene about ReiQ having Seele data might suggest that she may be manipulated by seele just like how kaworu was tricked in the series. There is great speculation about the pilot of eva 13 in the trailer, which ranges from Kaworu, Kaworu dummy plug and now based on what Reichu has suggested: ReiQ, who may potentially become programmed under seeles data to become an antagonist ( https://www.arqacrypha.net/post/rei-6-t ... -shouted-i ). With the absense of kaworu, seele may decide to use reiQ as a backup plan

[]: Re: Reichu's dissection of the ReiQ reveal scene             Quote Post by Heaven Piercing Man » Sun Jan 10, 2021 11:40 am The thing I'm most fixated about is the REI00 list. Wouldn't have lasted more than a couple of seconds yet it would have revealed so much. And it's frustrating how in the storyboard the KETSUBAN sign covers precisely the most important part.

[]: Re: Reichu's dissection of the ReiQ reveal scene             Quote Post by Zusuchan » Sun Jan 10, 2021 4:14 pm dzzthink: Seele is dead right now in NTE-Gendo and Fuyutsuki killed them. So whatever's going to happen in Shin, won't involve them as part of the equation.

[]: Re: Reichu's dissection of the ReiQ reveal scene             Quote Post by Kendrix » Tue Jan 12, 2021 12:47 am I'm convinced that they left some plan in place; They left cause they felt their physical presence was no longer needed, their faith in predestination is just that absolute (the motif of headless creatures is probably related to that) Indeed Gendo & Fuyu say that everything went exactly like their plan.

[]: Re: Reichu's dissection of the ReiQ reveal scene             Quote Post by JoelcrNeto » Tue Jan 12, 2021 6:55 am Yes, SEELE is dead. And I wonderer whether they've decided to follow Gendo, or they have a second plan. It's very strange that SEELE allowed Gendo to do this, and Kiel's last words, gave me the impression that they accepted this and that they seem to follow Gendo's plans.

[]: Re: Reichu's dissection of the ReiQ reveal scene             Quote Post by Derantor » Tue Jan 12, 2021 7:05 am @Kendrix: To be nitpicky, Fuyutsuki says "This is almost exactly what SEELE wished for", to which Gendo replies "It will suffice for now" (or something) - implying that they did indeed achieve their goal but just barely.

Article: Reichus dissection of the ReiQ reveal scene
[]: Reichu's dissection of the ReiQ reveal scene             Quote Post by Kendrix » Sat Dec 19, 2020 3:29 pm Now while Reichu's interpretations are at times far fetched & I disagree with her takes on some of the characters, she's very good at picking up meaningful details practically when it comes to the EVAs/angels/ "magic systems" & we MUST absolutely honor & appreciate how she took the time to do this. Both a parallel to ep 23, but also another example of how the "artificial soldier" related horror has been amped up here. They only need the heads, the brains... everything else has been dispensed with as uneccesary. Also, they obviously tie in with the various EVA component parts around Neo Nerv and the "green army" of EVAs in the trailer. Indeed in a cut scene dissected in the second part it's show that there's even more factory equipment in that room. Capsules & conveyor belts to box the finished dummy plugs. I feel proud of myself for including a factory of partially disassembled Reis in a fanfic now. A depressing thing that would have come through more if that had stayed in tho, is that it's not that Fuyutsuki has the Rei heads. in his chess room. He has set up his chess table in the Rei factory. Article: Reichus dissection of the ReiQ reveal scene
 can be deduced - she was sent & prepared by SEELE. This too can actually be deduced. Mark 9 is clearly SEELE's toy, and Mari calls her "SEELE pilot" though she could've been lumping Gendo & SEELE together. For the most part, Gendo didn't have any tools/pilots left that weren't foreign tools sent to fulfill SEELE's agenda. I was always surprised that we never got a version of "Q with a few little extra scenes added" like they did for the first two movies. My guess is that, just like they're holding out on the Q CR, they don't want to drop their "punchline". But I expect that IF it did happen, the extra bits of Fuyutsuki's explanation might have been included. I hope it will be. Now a lot of this probably still applies, it looks like a time/ centering cut. The existence of "interim Reis" is already heavily implied through that school uniform next to ReiQ's room. Then there's the very ambiguous use of the word"data". When Fuyutsuki said that "her data remains in the Rei clones" one would at first think that he's simply referring to her genes. It could just be some private desire of Gendo's to replicate her, or just a means (at least in the original, they didn't choose Yui as a donor; They used her lillith-contaminated "corpse", literally "salvaged remains". ) Maybe it was just as a means to "program" her at all, better copy something existent than build from scratch. Like how Naoko used herself as a base for the Magi. We've certainly heard that both the Magis and the Dummy plugs were a result of research into "artificial soul" But that's the original. If data doesn't just mean genes, what is it? Because it's not Yui's soul, she's in EVA 01. So far I've always been a proponent of the interpretation that Rei is ultimately meant to be her own person & in terms of character completely independent, a new person molded by a different life & environment - certainly her actions in wanting to make sure Shinji never has to pilot again vs. Yui making sure he absolutely does have to by refusing to accept the Dummy seem different enough. Still I must admit that. Though Rei could still have become her own person after starting with a similar "basis programming

ji never has to pilot again vs. Yui making sure he absolutely does have to by refusing to accept the Dummy seem different enough. Still I must admit that. Though Rei could still have become her own person after starting with a similar "basis programming/temperament" (irl where there is no eva specific magic, genes would already include base temperament), and that they tried to program her to be similar doesn't mean they necessarily succeeded to create more than an artists' impression of a Yui-like human personality. I don't even think the memory transfer made the original 3 "the same", but it could pertain to how her relationships with others are to be seen. For example the line of argument that "Shinji didn't befriend her because she resembled Yui, after all ReiQ is still a clone of her to the same extent" becomes a bit less certain now Did Yui undergo a brain scan/"tube session", or is that "data" something they got from her remains? It should be noted that all the pilots, even still living ones, leave some imprint that makes the EVAs "smell" like them (though since the sense of smell is the most connected to the unconscioous/emotions, itmight be something telepathic being "parsed" as a smell. ) and that the EVAs need to be configured for the pilot's "profiles". That's my best guess, if I had to chose one - they still had that "profile" from the contact experiment that that somehow went into Rei's "programming". Perhaps out of sentimentality, perhaps because they had to use something to get more than oversized infant. In the original series Rei was originally supposed to be EVA 01's pilot but in Rebuild Shinji was explicitly always intended as a pilot because of the whole "destiny manipulating" But leaving the question of how Yui is related to it aside for now (since we will probably see the intention from how things are framed in final), what's out of question is that they didn't bother programming ReiQ to the same extent Either SEELE wanted her to be more controllable, doesn't need her to show initiative since their program in Mark 9 will do that, or they had no need of anything Gendo added out of sentiment (though I still think that whatever the underlying undercurrent he must have had some technological reason to rationalize it with) Even in NGE the memory storage per tube didn't really transfer the complete personality, & its artificial

Article: Reichus dissection of the ReiQ reveal scene
 Gendo added out of sentiment (though I still think that whatever the underlying undercurrent he must have had some technological reason to rationalize it with) Even in NGE the memory storage per tube didn't really transfer the complete personality, & its artificiality was apparent in how it only went back to the last "save point" - so my theory re: how ReiQ is different from of Rei III (and they are very different indeed; Superficially they're both at first glance significantly less expressive than even early series Rei II, but if I had to describe them with two words, Rei III would be "angry ghost" and ReiQ "lost child") was just signal degradation. Copy of a copy of a copy... each time more unreliable. Something is left but not much - I based on this on how she seemed to have some idea who he is, & addresses him the same way, but it's only a grainy primitive recognition, more factual memory than biographic. But of course it's fully possible that Gendo just told her Shinji's name & she didn't have a preexisting notion of him at all, or, Seele thought basic knowledge his name/appearance could be left in. In NGE terms, it's like they decanted a clone and didn't load in the memories, or only a fraction of them. Certainly the most interesting part is where Reichu discusses two possible interpretations for the line relating to Gendo that he translated differently than Number-kun. "She doesn't have Yui's data so Gendo isn't interested" implies something waaaay different than "“Ikari can’t muster up enough interest in this clone to bother putting Yui’s data into it” The former is pretty 1D. Gendo is super callous, interested only in Yui, doesn't care about Rei and certainly not a Rei that's an even worse copy of her. there would even be the possibility to read this as implying that Shinji is basically the same as Gendo here (though for me, many more data points point to it instead being a validation of his "no one can replace you" line, especially with how the books come into it. Of course if we learn tomorrow that Yui was actually a huge bookworm, then it's time for me to redo my interpretation of the movies from scratch and probably pick a new favorite character.) The second one, however, is MUCH more... it's - It's so VERY Gendo-like , so much in

Article: Reichus dissection of the ReiQ reveal scene
 bookworm, then it's time for me to redo my interpretation of the movies from scratch and probably pick a new favorite character.) The second one, however, is MUCH more... it's - It's so VERY Gendo-like , so much in line with how I've always understood him. And that's the main reason I wish this had been left in since there's little else, besides that one office conversation, that provides any in Gendo, to begin with, is a character whose life is essentially "on hold" because he's never accepted Yui's death; Everything will be justified & fixed through instrumentality, so whatever he does in its name doesn't matter. He was jaded & somewhat overwhelmed by interpersonal matters to begin with, and the loss destroyed him - He can only pull up so much interest or emotional energy for the present. Not for Ritsuko, not for his children. He's friendly to Rei but it's still somewhat distant, faded, he doesn't really know what goes on in her head. He didn't choose to get attached to Rei, he couldn't help it because he's still made up of human brain chemicals. This is why I don#t really think he made Rei more similar to Yui on purpose, he doesn't do reminders he burned them all and he hopes to get the original from eva 01. He created her from the "salvaged remains" because that's the human SoL amalgam sample that he had. In 2.0 that seemed to be changing a bit, he was actually interacting with Shinji... But now like in all the other relationships, all the progress was erased. The timeskip must have been tough for Gendo too after all at the end of 2.22 he thought that it was "only a little longer". That didn't work out for him. At all. Unlike Misato & Asuka who--- yes they became bitter & didn't show their best sides to Shinji but aside from his specific case they are heroic defenders of humanity. They grew from their loss. Gendo has become even more callous, even colder, even more "dehumanized" butmostly even more burned out emotionally. He doesn't even try building new bonds. Perhaps the last few clones with the programming did have "copy of a copy" signal degradation; In any case he didn't bother to try again. He can't bring himself to care, he's reduced to apathy, it would be too painful, maybe. That

Article: Reichus dissection of the ReiQ reveal scene
 clones with the programming did have "copy of a copy" signal degradation; In any case he didn't bother to try again. He can't bring himself to care, he's reduced to apathy, it would be too painful, maybe. That's also why he only speaks with Shinji as briefly as possible. Interacting with him would also be too painful. Besides he's still going to use them so, better not get attached to the tools. The suggestion of other replicas is certainly a good catch, that might explain the Kaworu shots from the trailer Especially with the revelation that the fourth part is essentially two films in one, it seems the originally planned "third part" balooned and was split into two. (they weren't even sure where to make the cut, hence the existence of "until you come to me"); Or maybe the cut is where it is because Anno needed a break. Still, "right after Kaworu's death, ending with the trio wandering into the landscape" is a good, logical end for Q. The PLAQUE is also interesting & might suspect that there were actually a whole lot more If Mark six was piloted by another Rei that explains why the angel takes a Rei-ish form later on . (To ReiQ's horror) Or maybe that's the designation of the clone in its dummy plug (since Mark 6 is "autonomous"), so maybe not all of these were active Reis. Especially since "00" is assigned to "01" (remember the classic series dummy plug labeled "Rei 00"?) I'm not the only one who has speculated in fanfic about a non-functional protope "Rei 0". "Rei Infinity" is definitely the new head-only dummy plugs. Ep 23 had a panel listing all theclones from 4 to 24 and Rirsuko uses it to destroy them. One Rei has definitely made it to Lillith in some form. I mean, this would also explain/ mention the missing EVAs between 5 and 12 excluding 8. Also, I Think the R"Rei" in front of 00 is simply in uppercase, and those two dots are a badly written lowercase i. Or maybe they got lazy in the scrubbing and it would have all been written the same. Or, it's not Mark Six. It's Eva Six, the Marks & regular EVAs actually not having the same numbering. I'd though they just threw in extra EVAs and Reis tolampshade

Article: Reichus dissection of the ReiQ reveal scene
 would have all been written the same. Or, it's not Mark Six. It's Eva Six, the Marks & regular EVAs actually not having the same numbering. I'd though they just threw in extra EVAs and Reis tolampshade that much time has passed and that we'll never hear of them; just I didn't expect a clear rundown of what exactly happened (some time passed between NTI and actual TI, but EVA 01 must have been involved because of all the EVA 01 shaped things produced. ) Anyways, this is a goldmine, thanks Reichu, even if we don't get full answers we'll have fun speculating. Someone must try their hand at the timeskip chaos. Though that would be a lot like "EoTv but the physical events". Indeed I'm not even sure that the circle really means "active". We'd know if we could know how 06 looks. If it's circled it's active; If it's just written there without cross outs or circles, circles mean something else. Strikethrough is probably "dead", like Rei I. (Perhaps the long string of consecutive dead ones with no EVAs assigned had something to do with Ritsuko's defection to WILLE hehe) Circle could be " sealed ". So the list originally started out with no circles or strikethroughs. Rei 2 and the Mark Six Rei (if she existed) were both sealed/merged. (this would also fit the idea that Mark 9-like EVAs absorb their pilots, this happened to some other Reis, hence the circle, because they're now sealed. ) I'm so hype friends

[]: Re: Reichu's dissection of the ReiQ reveal scene             Quote Post by dzzthink » Sat Dec 26, 2020 7:23 am This begs the question of what the Yui data was even for and its purpose. It could be that its inherent nature of providing Yui's maternal 'smell' or familiarity is what makes Shinji so drawn to her in the first place, and that this was an overarching ploy by Nerv to make Shinji become desperate to save her and trigger 4th impact in the 2nd film. We know smell is key to identifying people you know of and provides a framework for developing relationships. It also makes some sense considering that Yui data was not provided for ReiQ leading to her loss of identity feeling. It also explains why Gendo has no interest whatsoever in interacting with ReiQ, who does not possess any of Yui's imprint. I think the description Rei III would be "angry ghost" and ReiQ "lost child" is a perfect description of the Rei clones. In contrast to Rei Q, Rei III does probably still have Yui's data and this gives her an intrinsic sense of who everyone is and their relationship to her. In this sense, it is the Yui data that has provided her motivations to choose Shinji over Gendo in EoE. Indeed, it is probable that ReiQ will be needed to trigger the instrumentality in the Rebuild movies and the omission of Yui's data, as you have said, was done for SEELE to make her more controllable. However, it is worthy to point out that Rei Q and Rei III both share the power of being able to hear Shinji calling out to Rei since in 3.33 Rei Q was able to locate Shinji on the Wille ship. This leads to another more interesting theory that for the next incarnation of Reis after Rei II, it is Yui's maternal instincts/emotions combined with possessing Lillith qualities that give some metaphysical third-eye sense of those around them (especially Shinji). Overall, it would be interesting to understand how much the Lillith part of

Article: Reichus dissection of the ReiQ reveal scene
, it is Yui's maternal instincts/emotions combined with possessing Lillith qualities that give some metaphysical third-eye sense of those around them (especially Shinji). Overall, it would be interesting to understand how much the Lillith part of Rei gives rise to her being and personality. Another question is whether she was created only upon Shinji reawakening to retrieve him from Wille. This again makes it difficult to understand how a clone can be created or grown using the technology they have. Rather than the typical embryo growth from fetus to child to teenager, it is likely that the formation of Rei relies on more unorthodox approaches. This makes it more likely that Rei Q was created only using biological data as a guide and Lilith's material for the rest. Then this psychological data is sort of an add-on that could be left out if needed. I agree with the concept of Rei being her own person, no matter what the incarnation is, and it is the environmental experiences and not Yui data or DNA that forms her personality. This is referenced heavily in episode 25 during Rei's monologue where she states that who she is, is based on the relationships she has formed and the experiences she has had. Then when she is asked whether she is afraid of her true self, we get flashes of the dummy plug reis', which suggests her original self was essentially an empty shell with no personality. This also suggests that the process of developing Rei clones all start off with physical replicas but with blank mental states. She is essentially afraid of being reverted back to nothing and lose everything that she has gained in her life to form her personality of Rei II. This is quite a tragedy later on when she dies and is no longer able to continue as her true self and build on what her life has been. However, based on the assumption that Rei is her own person and doesn't necessarily share any identity with Yui, then what is it that exactly is this Yui-like sense that governs Rei III's decision to choose Shinji over Gendo in EoE? Well, I have been thinking about this recently and I believe, that it is not necessarily a genetic or personal decision of Rei III but a program or instinct. You see, unit 01 is not Yui nor does it act like a human, yet it acts on some basic impulse or program to protect Shinji whenever possible. Yui would never have the capacity to start behaving like a berserk monster and start eating an angel or tear them apart limb from limb

Article: Reichus dissection of the ReiQ reveal scene
 does it act like a human, yet it acts on some basic impulse or program to protect Shinji whenever possible. Yui would never have the capacity to start behaving like a berserk monster and start eating an angel or tear them apart limb from limb, yet Unit 01 has the ability to act on a set of instructions guided by Yui's soul to do these violent acts. In the same way, although Rei III has never known Shinji or Gendo all that much, she acts out based on a program whether it be a soul or some set of unlocked genes (epigenetics even) that makes her do the things that she does without thought. It isn't necessarily a personal decision, but a natural reaction ordained by the possession of Yui's remaining psyche. I also admit there is another theory floating around about Rei III having Rei II's memory so make the decision to give the choice to Shinji, but I don't think this is necessarily accurate since Rei III seems to be different to Rei II (cracks gendo's glasses whilst Rei II during instrumentality picks up Gendo's glasses). There is also the manga (book 12 and 13), which also suggests Rei III remembering Rei II's interactions with Shinji and holding his hand but I'm not sure if they are following the same storyline or rather Sadamoto just added this for the sake of simplifying the Rei incarnation dynamics.

[]: Re: Reichu's dissection of the ReiQ reveal scene             Quote Post by dzzthink » Fri Jan 08, 2021 9:01 pm I want to stress again Reichu's previous analysis of 3.33 deleted scene about Yui's data being absent in Rei and Seele data being used instead. The question is why would the creators want to delete this important plot detail? I think it may have to do with not revealing seeles agenda so early on until shin eva and I want to draw a parallel with a similar deleted scene in the series, which is Kaworu's lake discussion with Seele. In that scene, we learn more about Seele's hidden agenda, as well as Kaworu's true identity as the vessel of Adam's soul. Seele also insists that they are on the angels side and consider them a more superior successor, almost as though to convince kaworu to go along with their plans. Kaworu, as we know, is Seele's golden boy, their last resort to thwarting Gendo's plans before they wage a full-on war against NERV in EoE. Just like how Gendo needs Rei

Article: Reichus dissection of the ReiQ reveal scene
 with their plans. Kaworu, as we know, is Seele's golden boy, their last resort to thwarting Gendo's plans before they wage a full-on war against NERV in EoE. Just like how Gendo needs Rei to carry out third impact, Seele uses Kaworu for their own purposes by tricking him to think that it is adam in the geofront. Why Kaworu seems to prefer Adam over Lillith is a mystery (aside from adam being the progenitor of angels and lillith being the progenitor of humans) but we know that his death as the final Angel as well as the removal of the spear of Longinus is a crucial step for Gendo to carry out his agenda in EoE, much to seeles dismay. Overall, this deletion of the seele data dialogue may have been done to not give away seeles agneda and their potential deception/manipulation of other characters. In the series, there are two separate agendas working against each other: Nerv and Seele, with seele pulling much of the strings with kaworus manipulation. In the Rebuild, there are now three factions at war against each other: Wille, Seele, and Nerv. The lines from the deleted scene about ReiQ having Seele data might suggest that she may be manipulated by seele just like how kaworu was tricked in the series. There is great speculation about the pilot of eva 13 in the trailer, which ranges from Kaworu, Kaworu dummy plug and now based on what Reichu has suggested: ReiQ, who may potentially become programmed under seeles data to become an antagonist ( https://www.arqacrypha.net/post/rei-6-t ... -shouted-i ). With the absense of kaworu, seele may decide to use reiQ as a backup plan
